title: Happy birthday poem (Feb 7th — Yahir & Dad)
html:
TL;DR

  • Dad and Yahir share a Feb 7 birthday; Yahir was the "regalo" — the gift his father swore not to fail.
  • The poem names the lineage explicitly: "I look like my mom / dad looks like his dad / a cycle that has no end."
  • Alcohol is the "evil spirit" that haunted the patriarchy across generations — vodka, whisky, rum.
  • Yahir's first generation guilt crystallizes here — "how can I be sad when I have so many opportunities?"
  • By 21 (Dad turning 50), birthdays had stopped being celebrated — implicit reference to the 2022 dad papers collapse.

The poem

A short, structured elegy. Yahir frames his existence as the patriarch's inheritance and counter-prediction at once: born on his father's birthday, Mexico left behind as the "father's sacrifice — a forfeit of his happiness for mine." The mother hides in his room from drunken violence when he is 7; at 17 he hides in the same room. The mirror is exact. He grieves "the bliss of ignorance of my childhood" and mourns the "scattered pieces of my family." Closes on the narcissist as mirror line: "burdened by the pain I've inherited, now turned into strength to change the narrative." This is the poem-form companion to the "cut from his cloth" line — same diagnosis, lyric register.

Connections

  • dad yahir · mom yahir · yahir family · lineage as fate
  • alcoholism in the family · first generation guilt · narcissist father as mirror
